state_title: The Hidden Stack: Measuring Inference Backend Effects in Reliable Large Language Model Evaluation
problem_statement: Identical LLM weights and identical prompts can produce different benchmark exact-match scores when run on different inference backends (HuggingFace Transformers, vLLM, TGI). Small reported method gains may reflect infrastructure variance, not model capability.
existing_weakness: Papers rarely treat inference backend as an experimental factor; scores are reported as single point estimates.
method_summary: Variance decomposition measuring backend share of benchmark variance on GSM8K, MuSiQue, StrategyQA, 2Wiki.
contributions: Backend as measurable confounder in LLM evaluation
Variance decomposition with backend variance ratios
Empirical negative result: correction does not improve accuracy

figure.title: Motivation
figure.objective: Create a photorealistic-quality academic framework ILLUSTRATION (rich raster scientific diagram, NOT flat vector art, NOT matplotlib, NOT SVG-style simple shapes). Match this exact layout style: three wide columns left-to-right with soft tinted rounded outer panels — light BLUE left, light ORANGE center with DASHED border, light GREEN right — subtle drop shadows, horizontal arrows between columns, small embedded region headers inside each panel top (NOT a full-width title). LEFT column header 'Fixed Inputs': top white rounded box with small server icon, label Fixed Model Block, text Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (frozen weights); bottom white rounded box with document icon, label Fixed Prompt Box, sample Q: What is the capital of Australia? A: (same template), subtitle Standardized Input. CENTER column header 'Inference Backends' inside dashed orange panel: three stacked backend cards exactly like a polished conference figure — BLUE card HuggingFace Transformers with bullet rows FP16 precision, Dynamic batching, Default stop rules; ORANGE card vLLM with BF16 precision, Continuous batching, Optimized CUDA kernels; GREEN card TGI with FP16 precision, Tensor parallelism, Custom decoding. Show backend SOFTWARE stacks only — do NOT draw tokenize-to-decode layer pipelines, speculative decoding, or latency icons. RIGHT column header 'Benchmark Outcome': top white subpanel with bar chart titled Exact Match Accuracy, three colored bars labeled HF 92.1%, vLLM 92.4%, TGI 91.8%; below chart three small answer snippet boxes (color-matched) showing same question with slightly different phrasing, one differing word in red. Tiny corner legend bottom-right only: Blue HF, Orange vLLM, Green TGI. STRICT RULES: NO figure title at top of image; NO bottom banner or motivational sentence; NEVER use ampersand character anywhere (write 'and' instead); NO Backend A/B/C generic names; NO latency, throughput, GPU utilization, parallel-decode, or system optimization story; NO data cleaning or ML training pipeline content.
figure.caption: Identical model and prompt, different inference backends, divergent benchmark scores.
figure.data_source: introduction motivation figure for ACL two-column paper
